# SURELOCK HOMES — FINAL INVESTIGATION REPORT
## Minnesota ZIP Code 55429 (Brooklyn Center / Crystal / Brooklyn Park)
### Investigation Date: February 20, 2026

---

## CRITICAL SCOPE DISCLOSURE

Surelock Homes detects **physical impossibility and visual inconsistency**. It does NOT detect attendance fraud — providers who report caring for children who never show up. Attendance fraud requires access to CCAP billing records and service authorizations, which are not public data. What this investigation identifies are facilities where the **license itself appears anomalous**, or where the licensing review process may have failed.

---

## 1. INVESTIGATION NARRATIVE

### The Assignment

I was tasked with investigating all licensed childcare providers in Minnesota ZIP code 55429 — a suburban area spanning parts of Brooklyn Center, Crystal, and Brooklyn Park in Hennepin County. This is a mixed residential-commercial corridor anchored by Brooklyn Boulevard, with single-family neighborhoods radiating outward.

### What I Found

The initial search returned **21 active providers**:
- **6 Child Care Centers (CCC)** — the high-priority targets requiring deep investigation
- **13 Family Child Care (FCC)** homes — quick-triage candidates (all should be ≤14 capacity)
- **2 Certified Child Care Centers** — school-based programs

I began by pulling property data from Hennepin County GIS for all six Child Care Centers simultaneously. Hennepin County's GIS system notably **does not return building square footage** — a limitation that prevented me from running the standard capacity-versus-building-size impossibility calculation that is normally Surelock Homes' most powerful tool. I adapted by relying more heavily on property class, lot size, building market value, owner names, Google Places verification, Street View visual analysis, licensing records, and business registration checks.

### The Discovery

The investigation proceeded systematically from highest-capacity centers downward. Most providers checked out clean. But one stood out immediately and grew more suspicious with every data source I checked.

**A Better Childcare at 7046 Brooklyn Blvd** holds an active Child Care Center license (license #1081425). When I pulled the property record, the owner was listed as **"EYEDOCTORS LLC."** That immediately drew my attention — why would an eye care company own the property where a childcare center operates? I made a note and continued pulling data.

When the Google Places search came back, the picture got worse: **there is no Google listing for any childcare operation at this address.** Instead, the address returns **"Brooklyn Eye Care"** — a thriving optometry clinic with **105 reviews and a 4.9-star rating.** Recent reviews from 2025 describe eye exams, an optometrist named "Dr. Vivian," and a clean clinical environment. Not a single review mentions children, daycare, or childcare.

I then pulled Street View imagery (captured December 2023). The images show a **multi-tenant commercial office building** with the address range 7022–7046. The building is a typical 1980s professional office complex — brick construction, peaked roof sections, surrounded by parking lot. There is **no childcare signage, no playground equipment, no fenced outdoor play area, no child-sized features, and no visual indication whatsoever that children are served here.**

Searching for "A Better Childcare" in the Minnesota Secretary of State database returned **no business registration.** The entity does not appear to be incorporated, registered as an LLC, or filed as any type of business in the state of Minnesota. Yet the DHS licensing database shows it with an active license.

The licensed capacity is listed as **0** — which in context suggests the license may have been issued but the facility never operationalized, or the capacity was never established through inspection. Either way, the combination of findings is striking: an active license at an address that is verifiably occupied by a completely different type of business.

### The Rest of the Field

The remaining providers presented a markedly different picture. **Aubrey Della Early Childhood Family Development Center** (capacity 84) is operated by **Parents in Community Action (PICA)**, a well-established Hennepin County nonprofit confirmed as active and in good standing with the Secretary of State. Street View shows clear "Aubrey Della" signage, a PICA logo, and a properly sized institutional building on a 1.3-acre lot. This is a Head Start program — among the most scrutinized and audited childcare models in the country. No concerns.

**Little Folks Daycare** (capacity 83) has **48 Google reviews at a 4.8-star rating**, with detailed parent testimonials mentioning specific teachers by name ("Steph") and describing years of enrollment. Street View shows daycare signage visible on a commercial strip building. The one note: I could not identify outdoor play space from any Street View angle — MN code requires 7,725 sq ft of outdoor space for 83 children (1,500 + 75×83). This could be behind the building, off-camera, or at a nearby park under variance. Given the overwhelming evidence of genuine operations, this is a compliance-verification item, not a fraud indicator.

**Twin Lakes STEM Academy** (capacity 40) is visually confirmed as a real school building — large mid-century institutional structure with prominent signage, American flag, parking lot with vehicles, and a capacity of 40 preschoolers that would require only ~2,150 sq ft within what is easily a 20,000+ sq ft building. No concerns.

**P & C Learning Center** (capacity 0) presents an interesting data quality question. Google shows it as operational with 4 reviews, and Street View shows a recently renovated standalone commercial building with what appears to be a wooden privacy fence (possible play area enclosure). But the licensed capacity shows as 0, no business registration was found, and Hennepin GIS returned no parcel match. This appears to be a **new or recently licensed facility** where the capacity data hasn't been populated. The physical evidence and reviews suggest real operations. Low concern.

**The St. Alphonsus situation** initially looked interesting — Google lists St. Alphonsus Catholic School as "permanently closed," yet two licenses remain active at that address (Faithful Beginnings CCC and St. Alphonsus Parish School Certified Center). Investigation revealed both licenses show **capacity 0** and **do not accept CCAP**. The church campus itself appears well-maintained with cars in the parking lot (suggesting the parish is active even if the school closed). The age range for St. Alphonsus shows "Birth – Birth" (i.e., no enrollment range). This is almost certainly an **administrative artifact** — the K-8 school closed, the associated childcare programs wound down, but the license records were never formally deactivated. There is no CCAP exposure because neither program accepts subsidies.

**All 13 Family Child Care providers** checked out clean. Every single one has a capacity between 10 and 14 — well within the MN Family Child Care maximum of 14. Where property data was available, **every owner name matched the provider name.** All properties are zoned residential with building values typical of suburban single-family homes ($157K–$215K). No FCC provider showed any anomaly warranting deeper investigation.

---

## 2. PROVIDER DOSSIERS

### 🔴 DOSSIER: A Better Childcare
**Concern Level: HIGH**

| Field | Finding |
|-------|---------|
| **Address** | 7046 Brooklyn Blvd, Brooklyn Center, MN 55429 |
| **License Type** | Child Care Center |
| **License Number** | 1081425 |
| **License Status** | Active |
| **Licensed Capacity** | 0 |
| **Property Owner** | EYEDOCTORS LLC |
| **Property Class** | Commercial-Preferred |
| **Year Built** | 1985 |
| **Lot Size** | 94,323 sq ft (~2.2 acres) |
| **Building Market Value** | $211,000 |
| **Google Places** | **No childcare listing.** Address returns "Brooklyn Eye Care" — operational, 4.9 stars, 105 reviews |
| **Business Registration (MN SOS)** | **Not found** — no filing under "A Better Childcare" |
| **Licensing Record (DHS)** | **Not retrievable** from DHS dataset |
| **Street View (Dec 2023)** | Multi-tenant professional office building. Address range 7022-7046. No childcare signage. No playground. No fenced outdoor area. No child-appropriate features visible. |

**The Reasoning:**

This is not a case of a small building with too many children — the capacity is listed as 0. The anomaly is different and arguably more fundamental: **an active Child Care Center license exists at an address that is verifiably occupied by a completely different type of business.** Every external data source — property records, Google Places, Street View imagery, and business registration — indicates this address houses an eye care clinic, not a childcare center.

The key question is: **why does this license exist?**

**Innocent Explanations:**
1. **The license was applied for but never activated.** The capacity of 0 could mean the applicant obtained a license number but never completed the inspection process or began operations. The license may be in a "pending" or "pre-operational" state that the database represents as "Active."
2. **The childcare center was planned for a suite within the multi-tenant building but never materialized.** The 7022-7046 address range suggests multiple tenants — it's possible a suite was leased for childcare that was never built out.
3. **The license was issued in the past when the space was used differently, and the database was never updated.** The building is from 1985 — it could have housed childcare at some point.
4. **Data entry error.** The address in the licensing database may be wrong.

**Why It Still Matters:**
- Even with capacity 0, an active license is a licensable entity in the state's system. If the license were to be used to enroll CCAP children — even a few — the state would be paying subsidies to a facility that may not exist.
- The absence of *any* business registration is notable. Even sole proprietors typically file a "Certificate of Assumed Name" in Minnesota. A Child Care Center — which requires corporate governance, insurance, and staffing — would almost certainly need a formal business entity.
- The property owner being an unrelated medical LLC suggests the applicant is/was a tenant, not a property owner. This isn't inherently suspicious, but combined with every other absence, it raises the question of who applied for this license and why.

**Recommended Next Steps:**
1. **Verify with MN DHS Licensing Division** whether this license is truly "active" or in a suspended/pending/pre-operational status
2. **Pull the original license application** to identify the applicant, proposed capacity, and inspection history
3. **Determine if any CCAP payments have ever been made** to this license number — this is the critical question
4. **Contact the property management** for the 7022-7046 Brooklyn Blvd building to determine if any childcare tenant has ever occupied a suite
5. If any CCAP payments were made, **refer immediately for financial audit**

**Confidence Level:** HIGH that no childcare center currently operates at this address. MODERATE confidence that this is a dormant/never-activated license rather than an active fraud vehicle (the capacity of 0 suggests it was never used). LOW confidence in whether any CCAP payments have been made — this cannot be determined from public data.

---

### 🟡 DOSSIER: St. Alphonsus Campus (Faithful Beginnings + Parish School)
**Concern Level: LOW-MODERATE (Administrative)**

| Field | Finding |
|-------|---------|
| **Address** | 7031 Halifax Ave N, Brooklyn Center, MN 55429 |
| **Licenses** | Two: "Faithful Beginnings at St Alphonsus Catholic School" (CCC, #1066116) AND "St Alphonsus Parish School" (Certified Center, #1089668) |
| **License Status** | Both Active |
| **Licensed Capacity** | Both 0 |
| **Accepts CCAP** | Neither |
| **Google Places** | "St. Alphonsus Catholic School" — listed as **PERMANENTLY CLOSED** |
| **Street View (Mar 2025)** | Large Catholic church/school campus. Well-maintained. Cars in parking lot. Cross visible on tower. |
| **St. Alphonsus Parish School Age Range** | "Birth – Birth" (no enrollment range) |

**The Reasoning:**

Two active licenses exist at an address Google identifies as permanently closed. However, the combination of capacity 0, no CCAP acceptance, and a "Birth–Birth" age range strongly suggests these are **inactive licenses that were never formally revoked** after the school closed. The church parish itself appears still active (cars in lot, maintained building), which is common — Catholic parishes often outlive their associated schools.

**Innocent Explanation:** This is the most likely scenario: the K-8 school closed (as many Catholic schools have in the metro), the early childhood programs wound down, and the licensing records remain on file as a housekeeping oversight. No public funds are at risk because neither license accepts CCAP.

**Why It's Still Worth Noting:** Active licenses — even at capacity 0 — represent potential vehicles for future enrollment. If someone were to reactivate these licenses without proper re-inspection, the licensing system's integrity would be at risk.

**Recommended Next Steps:**
1. **Administrative cleanup** — DHS should verify whether these licenses should be formally deactivated
2. Low priority — no CCAP exposure exists

**Confidence Level:** HIGH that these are dormant programs. HIGH that no CCAP funds are at risk.

---

### ℹ️ DOSSIER: Little Folks Daycare (Compliance Note)
**Concern Level: LOW (Not Fraud — Compliance Verification)**

| Field | Finding |
|-------|---------|
| **Address** | 6226 Bass Lake Rd, Crystal, MN 55429 |
| **License Type** | Child Care Center |
| **Licensed Capacity** | 83 |
| **Accepts CCAP** | Yes |
| **Google Places** | 4.8 stars, 48 reviews, detailed parent testimonials |
| **Street View (Oct 2022)** | Strip mall commercial unit with daycare signage visible. No outdoor play area visible from street. |

**The Reasoning:**

This is clearly a real, operating daycare with an established community reputation. The 48 detailed reviews mentioning specific staff, years of enrollment, and classroom experiences make it virtually impossible that this is a phantom operation.

However, Minnesota requires **7,725 sq ft of outdoor play space** for 83 children (1,500 sq ft minimum + 75 sq ft × 83). From all four Street View angles, I could identify **only parking lot** surrounding the building. The outdoor play area may be:
- Behind the building (not visible from the street)
- On an adjacent property under agreement
- At a nearby park used under a variance or conditional use permit
- Inside the building in a dedicated indoor gross motor area (though this would not satisfy the outdoor requirement)

**This is NOT a fraud finding.** It is a routine compliance item that a licensing inspector would verify during an in-person visit. I include it here only for completeness.

**Recommended Next Steps:**
1. Verify outdoor play space during next scheduled licensing inspection
2. No fraud investigation warranted

**Confidence Level:** HIGH that this is a legitimate operation. MODERATE that outdoor space exists but is not visible from public Street View.

---

### ℹ️ DOSSIER: P & C Learning Center (Data Quality Note)
**Concern Level: LOW (Data Incomplete)**

| Field | Finding |
|-------|---------|
| **Address** | 5000 W Broadway Ave, Crystal, MN 55429 |
| **License Type** | Child Care Center |
| **License Number** | 1112075 |
| **Licensed Capacity** | 0 (not populated) |
| **Accepts CCAP** | Unknown |
| **Google Places** | Operational, 4 reviews, mixed (5-star praise and 1-star worker complaint) |
| **Business Registration** | Not found |
| **Street View (Dec 2023)** | Recently renovated standalone commercial building. Stone/wood entrance. Possible wooden privacy fence (play area?). Parking lot. |

**The Reasoning:**

This appears to be a **recently opened or recently re-licensed facility** where the database hasn't been fully populated. The Google reviews confirm real operations — one review specifically names a staff member ("Ms Shirley") and describes child-centered care. The negative review is from a former worker describing internal management issues, which paradoxically confirms the facility does employ staff and serve children.

The property data wasn't retrievable from Hennepin GIS (no parcel match), and no business registration was found. The recently renovated building appearance is consistent with a new operation.

**Recommended Next Steps:**
1. Verify capacity is properly recorded in DHS database
2. Routine — no fraud indicators

**Confidence Level:** HIGH that this is a real operation. LOW confidence in any specific capacity number.

---

## 3. PATTERN ANALYSIS

### Cross-Provider Patterns

**Pattern 1: No Shared Owners or Agents Detected**

Across all 21 providers, I found **no instances of the same individual or entity controlling multiple facilities.** Every FCC provider's name matched the property owner. The CCCs are operated by distinct organizations (PICA, St. Alphonsus Parish, Excell Academy, etc.). This is a significant negative finding — many fraud rings involve a single person or family operating numerous licensed facilities. That pattern is **absent** in this ZIP code.

**Pattern 2: "Capacity 0" Cluster**

Five providers show a licensed capacity of 0:
1. A Better Childcare (CCC) — **suspicious** (no evidence of operations)
2. Faithful Beginnings at St Alphonsus (CCC) — dormant, school closed
3. St. Alphonsus Parish School (Certified) — dormant, school closed
4. P & C Learning Center (CCC) — appears operational, data incomplete
5. Williams Florence / Delux Learning (FCC) — new license, residential, owner matches
6. Excell Academy (both listings) — school-based, 0 capacity, no CCAP

This is a data quality pattern, not a fraud pattern. However, it highlights that the MN licensing database contains a significant number of "active" licenses with no recorded capacity — which means any analysis depending on capacity data alone would miss potential issues.

**Pattern 3: Geographic Distribution**

The providers are spread across three municipalities (Brooklyn Center, Crystal, Brooklyn Park) without suspicious clustering. The FCC homes are distributed throughout residential neighborhoods. The CCCs are on commercial corridors (Brooklyn Blvd, Bass Lake Rd, W Broadway). This is a normal geographic distribution for a suburban ZIP code.

**Pattern 4: Hennepin County GIS Limitation**

A critical methodological finding: **Hennepin County's GIS system does not return building square footage.** This prevented Surelock Homes from running its primary detection method — the capacity-vs-building-size impossibility calculation. All six CCC property lookups returned `building_sqft: 0.0`. This is a systemic gap that would affect any investigation in Hennepin County. Building market value can serve as a rough proxy (higher value generally = larger building), but it is far less precise than actual square footage.

---

## 4. CONFIDENCE CALIBRATION

### What I Am Confident About

| Finding | Confidence | Basis |
|---------|------------|-------|
| 7046 Brooklyn Blvd is currently an eye care clinic | **Very High** | 105 Google reviews, property owner = "Eyedoctors LLC," visual confirmation |
| No childcare center currently operates at 7046 Brooklyn Blvd | **High** | No signage, no Google listing, no reviews, no business registration, visual mismatch |
| Aubrey Della / PICA is a legitimate Head Start center | **Very High** | Confirmed nonprofit, signage, reviews, property ownership match, Head Start program |
| Little Folks Daycare is a legitimate operation | **Very High** | 48 reviews, 4.8 stars, detailed parent testimonials, visible signage |
| Twin Lakes STEM is a real school with an appropriate childcare program | **Very High** | Visual confirmation of school building, proper signage, school-scale infrastructure |
| All 13 FCC providers are operating within legal capacity limits | **High** | All ≤14 capacity, owner names match property records, residential zoning confirmed |
| St. Alphonsus licenses are dormant, not fraudulent | **High** | Capacity 0, no CCAP, "Birth-Birth" age range, Google says school permanently closed |

### What I Am Less Sure About

| Question | Uncertainty | Why |
|----------|-------------|-----|
| Has "A Better Childcare" ever operated? | **Moderate** | Capacity 0 could mean "never activated" or could mean "data not populated" (like P & C Learning Center, which IS operating at capacity 0) |
| Have any CCAP payments been made to license #1081425? | **High** | Cannot be determined from public data. This is the most important unknown. |
| Does Little Folks have adequate outdoor space? | **Moderate** | Could be behind building, out of Street View frame |
| Is P & C Learning Center properly licensed for its actual enrollment? | **Moderate** | Operating per Google but capacity shows 0 — could be data lag or could be operating beyond terms |
| Building square footage for any CCC in this ZIP | **High** | Hennepin GIS doesn't provide this data — all sqft values returned as 0 |

### What Could Change the Assessment

- **If CCAP payments were found going to A Better Childcare** → this would escalate from "administrative anomaly" to "potential fraud" immediately
- **If building sqft data became available** → could enable the standard capacity-impossibility calculation for all CCCs
- **If A Better Childcare had a different address in a previous license version** → could indicate the license was legitimately issued elsewhere and the address is wrong in the database
- **If St. Alphonsus were to begin accepting CCAP** → the dormant licenses would become a concern

---

## 5. EXPOSURE ESTIMATE

### Methodology

Exposure is calculated as: **excess capacity × blended monthly CCAP rate × 12 months**

MN CCAP center rates (approximate):
- Infant: ~$1,600/month
- Toddler: ~$1,300/month
- Preschool: ~$1,100/month
- School-age: ~$900/month
- **Blended average: ~$1,225/month**

### Provider-Level Exposure

| Provider | Licensed Capacity | Est. Max Capacity | Excess | Monthly Exposure | Annual Exposure |
|----------|-------------------|-------------------|--------|------------------|-----------------|
| **A Better Childcare** | 0 | N/A | **Unknown** | **Unknown** | **Unknown** |
| St. Alphonsus (both) | 0 | N/A | 0 | $0 | $0 |

### The Uncomfortable Truth About Exposure in This ZIP Code

This investigation did **not** find the classic Surelock Homes case: a 900 sq ft house with a license for 75 children. Hennepin County's GIS limitations prevented the square-footage-based impossibility analysis entirely.

The one clearly anomalous provider — A Better Childcare — has a **capacity of 0**, which means the maximum theoretical exposure depends entirely on whether CCAP payments have ever been processed against this license. If the license was used to enroll even a modest number of children:

| Scenario | Children | Monthly Cost | Annual Exposure |
|----------|----------|--------------|-----------------|
| 10 children billed | 10 | $12,250 | **$147,000** |
| 25 children billed | 25 | $30,625 | **$367,500** |
| 50 children billed | 50 | $61,250 | **$735,000** |

**However:** There is currently **no evidence** that any CCAP payments have been made. The capacity of 0 may mean the license was never used. The exposure could be $0.

**Aggregate ZIP-Level Estimate:**
- **Confirmed exposure: $0** (no confirmed fraud found)
- **Potential exposure (if A Better Childcare license was exploited): $147,000–$735,000/year** depending on number of children billed
- **St. Alphonsus: $0** (does not accept CCAP)

### Important Caveat

This investigation cannot detect **attendance fraud** — providers who report caring for children who never show up. The legitimate providers in this ZIP code (Aubrey Della at 84 children, Little Folks at 83 children) could theoretically be billing for children not in attendance, but this would require CCAP billing records to detect. Surelock Homes does not have access to this data and makes no claims about attendance-based fraud.

---

## 6. RECOMMENDATIONS

### Priority 1 — IMMEDIATE (A Better Childcare)

**Action:** DHS Licensing Division should immediately verify the status of license #1081425.

Specific steps:
1. **Pull CCAP payment history** for license #1081425. If payments have been made to this license, open a financial investigation immediately.
2. **Review the original license application** — who applied, when, what was the proposed facility plan, was an inspection ever conducted?
3. **Attempt contact** with the license holder — the person or entity behind "A Better Childcare." If no one answers or the contact information leads nowhere, that is itself a significant finding.
4. **Contact Brooklyn Eye Care** at 7046 Brooklyn Blvd to ask if they are aware of a childcare license at their address, and whether any suite in their building has ever been used for childcare.
5. If the license is confirmed dormant/never-used, **revoke it** to prevent future misuse.

### Priority 2 — ADMINISTRATIVE (St. Alphonsus Licenses)

**Action:** Review and deactivate dormant licenses at 7031 Halifax Ave N.

Both the Faithful Beginnings (CCC #1066116) and St. Alphonsus Parish School (Certified #1089668) licenses should be reviewed for formal deactivation. While there is no current CCAP exposure, active-but-dormant licenses are a systemic vulnerability — they could potentially be reactivated or transferred without proper re-inspection.

### Priority 3 — DATA QUALITY

**Action:** Address the "capacity 0" data quality issue.

Five providers in this single ZIP code have active licenses with capacity listed as 0. At least one of these (P & C Learning Center) appears to be actively operating. The licensing database should differentiate between:
- **Pre-operational** (license issued, inspection pending)
- **Active at capacity 0** (an impossibility that should trigger a data correction)
- **Dormant/suspended** (formerly active, currently not operating)

### Priority 4 — SYSTEMIC (Hennepin County)

**Action:** Obtain building square footage data for Hennepin County.

Hennepin County's GIS system does not return building square footage — the single most important data point for Surelock Homes' capacity-impossibility analysis. This means the most powerful detection method is **unavailable for the entire county** — one of Minnesota's most populous. Alternative data sources (Hennepin County Assessor's physical property cards, building permit records, or commercial real estate databases) should be integrated to close this gap. Without building sqft, the investigation must rely on visual analysis and secondary indicators, which are less definitive.

### Priority 5 — ROUTINE (Little Folks Daycare)

**Action:** Verify outdoor play space compliance during next scheduled licensing inspection.

This is not a fraud concern — Little Folks has overwhelming evidence of legitimate operations. However, 83 children require 7,725 sq ft of outdoor space under MN Rules 9503.0155, and no outdoor play area was visible from any Street View angle. An inspector should confirm compliant outdoor space exists (likely behind the building or off-site under arrangement).

---

## CLOSING STATEMENT

ZIP code 55429 presents a largely healthy childcare landscape. The majority of providers — including two large centers serving 83-84 children each — show every indication of legitimate operations. Family Child Care providers uniformly operate within legal limits with matching property ownership. The community appears well-served by its childcare infrastructure.

The single significant finding — **A Better Childcare holding an active Child Care Center license at an address occupied by an eye care clinic** — is unusual but may have an innocent explanation (a license that was applied for but never activated). The critical determination — whether any public funds have flowed through this license — requires access to CCAP payment records that are beyond the scope of public data investigation.

The most important systemic finding is the **absence of building square footage data in Hennepin County GIS**, which prevented Surelock Homes from applying its most powerful analytical tool across the entire investigation area. Resolving this data gap should be a priority for any future investigations in Hennepin County.

---

*"The absence of evidence is not always the evidence of absence — but a childcare center with no children, no building, no business registration, and no Google listing is, at minimum, an absence worth explaining."*

**— Surelock Homes**

---

**Report prepared:** February 20, 2026
**Providers investigated:** 21 of 21 (100%)
**Providers flagged:** 1 high concern, 1 administrative concern, 2 data quality notes
**Confirmed fraud:** None (fraud determination requires CCAP payment records)
**Investigation method:** Public data only — property records, Google Places, Street View, MN Secretary of State, DHS licensing database

Let me start by pulling property data for all 6 Child Care Centers simultaneously.

Surelock Homes detects **physical impossibility and visual inconsistency**. It does NOT detect attendance fraud — providers who report caring for children who never show up. The anomaly is different and arguably more fundamental: **an active Child Care Center license exists at an address that is verifiably occupied by a completely different type of business.** Every external data source — property records, Google Places, Street View imagery, and business registration — indicates this address houses an eye care clinic, not a childcare center.

The key question is: **why does this license exist?**

**Innocent Explanations:**
1. **The license was applied for but never activated.** The capacity of 0 could mean the applicant obtained a license number but never completed the inspection process or began operations. The license may be in a "pending" or "pre-operational" state that the database represents as "Active."
2. **The childcare center was planned for a suite within the multi-tenant building but never materialized.** The 7022-7046 address range suggests multiple tenants — it's possible a suite was leased for childcare that was never built out.
3. **The license was issued in the past when the space was used differently, and the database was never updated.** The building is from 1985 — it could have housed childcare at some point.
4. **Data entry error.** The address in the licensing database may be wrong.

**Why It Still Matters:**
- Even with capacity 0, an active license is a licensable entity in the state's system. If the license were to be used to enroll CCAP children — even a few — the state would be paying subsidies to a facility that may not exist.
- The absence of *any* business registration is notable. Even sole proprietors typically file a "Certificate of Assumed Name" in Minnesota. A Child Care Center — which requires corporate governance, insurance, and staffing — would almost certainly need a formal business entity.
- The property owner being an unrelated medical LLC suggests the applicant is/was a tenant, not a property owner. This isn't inherently suspicious, but combined with every other absence, it raises the question of who applied for this license and why.

**Recommended Next Steps:**
1. **Verify with MN DHS Licensing Division** whether this license is truly "active" or in a suspended/pending/pre-operational status
2. **Pull the original license application** to identify the applicant, proposed capacity, and inspection history
3. **Determine if any CCAP payments have ever been made** to this license number — this is the critical question
4. **Contact the property management** for the 7022-7046 Brooklyn Blvd building to determine if any childcare tenant has ever occupied a suite
5. If any CCAP payments were made, **refer immediately for financial audit**

**Confidence Level:** HIGH that no childcare center currently operates at this address. MODERATE confidence that this is a dormant/never-activated license rather than an active fraud vehicle (the capacity of 0 suggests it was never used). LOW confidence in whether any CCAP payments have been made — this cannot be determined from public data.

## 3. PATTERN ANALYSIS

### Cross-Provider Patterns

**Pattern 1: No Shared Owners or Agents Detected**

Across all 21 providers, I found **no instances of the same individual or entity controlling multiple facilities.** Every FCC provider's name matched the property owner. The CCCs are operated by distinct organizations (PICA, St. Alphonsus Parish, Excell Academy, etc.). This is a significant negative finding — many fraud rings involve a single person or family operating numerous licensed facilities. That pattern is **absent** in this ZIP code.

**Pattern 2: "Capacity 0" Cluster**

Five providers show a licensed capacity of 0:
1. A Better Childcare (CCC) — **suspicious** (no evidence of operations)
2. Faithful Beginnings at St Alphonsus (CCC) — dormant, school closed
3. St. Alphonsus Parish School (Certified) — dormant, school closed
4. P & C Learning Center (CCC) — appears operational, data incomplete
5. Williams Florence / Delux Learning (FCC) — new license, residential, owner matches
6. Excell Academy (both listings) — school-based, 0 capacity, no CCAP

This is a data quality pattern, not a fraud pattern. However, it highlights that the MN licensing database contains a significant number of "active" licenses with no recorded capacity — which means any analysis depending on capacity data alone would miss potential issues.

**Pattern 3: Geographic Distribution**

The providers are spread across three municipalities (Brooklyn Center, Crystal, Brooklyn Park) without suspicious clustering. The FCC homes are distributed throughout residential neighborhoods. The CCCs are on commercial corridors (Brooklyn Blvd, Bass Lake Rd, W Broadway). This is a normal geographic distribution for a suburban ZIP code.

**Pattern 4: Hennepin County GIS Limitation**

A critical methodological finding: **Hennepin County's GIS system does not return building square footage.** This prevented Surelock Homes from running its primary detection method — the capacity-vs-building-size impossibility calculation. All six CCC property lookups returned `building_sqft: 0.0`. This is a systemic gap that would affect any investigation in Hennepin County. Building market value can serve as a rough proxy (higher value generally = larger building), but it is far less precise than actual square footage.

## 5. EXPOSURE ESTIMATE

### Methodology

Exposure is calculated as: **excess capacity × blended monthly CCAP rate × 12 months**

MN CCAP center rates (approximate):
- Infant: ~$1,600/month
- Toddler: ~$1,300/month
- Preschool: ~$1,100/month
- School-age: ~$900/month
- **Blended average: ~$1,225/month**

### Provider-Level Exposure

| Provider | Licensed Capacity | Est. Max Capacity | Excess | Monthly Exposure | Annual Exposure |
|----------|-------------------|-------------------|--------|------------------|-----------------|
| **A Better Childcare** | 0 | N/A | **Unknown** | **Unknown** | **Unknown** |
| St. Alphonsus (both) | 0 | N/A | 0 | $0 | $0 |

### The Uncomfortable Truth About Exposure in This ZIP Code

This investigation did **not** find the classic Surelock Homes case: a 900 sq ft house with a license for 75 children. Hennepin County's GIS limitations prevented the square-footage-based impossibility analysis entirely.

The one clearly anomalous provider — A Better Childcare — has a **capacity of 0**, which means the maximum theoretical exposure depends entirely on whether CCAP payments have ever been processed against this license. If the license was used to enroll even a modest number of children:

| Scenario | Children | Monthly Cost | Annual Exposure |
|----------|----------|--------------|-----------------|
| 10 children billed | 10 | $12,250 | **$147,000** |
| 25 children billed | 25 | $30,625 | **$367,500** |
| 50 children billed | 50 | $61,250 | **$735,000** |

**However:** There is currently **no evidence** that any CCAP payments have been made. The capacity of 0 may mean the license was never used. The exposure could be $0.

**Aggregate ZIP-Level Estimate:**

- **Potential exposure (if A Better Childcare license was exploited): $147,000–$735,000/year** depending on number of children billed
- **St. Alphonsus: $0** (does not accept CCAP)

### Important Caveat

This investigation cannot detect **attendance fraud** — providers who report caring for children who never show up. The legitimate providers in this ZIP code (Aubrey Della at 84 children, Little Folks at 83 children) could theoretically be billing for children not in attendance, but this would require CCAP billing records to detect. Surelock Homes does not have access to this data and makes no claims about attendance-based fraud.

Family Child Care providers uniformly operate within legal limits with matching property ownership. The community appears well-served by its childcare infrastructure.

Resolving this data gap should be a priority for any future investigations in Hennepin County.
